steps.title: Spot the key contrast
steps.content: The passage opens by noting what people typically think about feathered dinosaurs (winged types like Yi qi), then pivots with “However” to introduce the main point: feathered dinosaurs also included wingless varieties. This contrast structure reveals the passage's central topic.

steps.title: Match the main topic
steps.content:
The passage establishes that dinosaurs with feathers were more widespread than commonly perceived—examples show feathering in both winged and wingless types. The other three choices either describe the opposite (dinosaurs without feathers), mention something never discussed (fossil value), or address an unrelated subject (birds).

That matches choice A — A is correct.
